shot_notes: The non-magical pledge and homebound promise
evidence_summary: Establish the vulnerability of Oz by stripping away his magical facade through a grounded, intimate confession. The scene must transition from high-tension confrontation to a shared sense of melancholy and cautious hope as the characters process his identit...

In a smaller, more intimate chamber, Oz explains his true identity as a balloonist from Omaha. He admits he has no magic but promises to fulfill his obligations (brains, heart, courage) through non-magical means and pledges to help Dorothy find a way home.
Tense confrontation within a confined room.
Quiet resignation and a tentative pact for future action.
Oz confesses his true identity as a balloonist from Omaha and promises non-magical solutions to the companions' needs.
